san-juan.title: San Juan
san-juan.tagline: The Historic Heart of Puerto Rico
san-juan.gettingThere: Fly into Luis Muñoz Marín International Airport (SJU) — major hub with direct flights from most US cities, no passport required for US citizens.

essentials.gettingAround: Uber and local taxis work well. Rent a car for day trips outside the city.
ferryConnections: Vieques (1.5 hr ferry from Ceiba, $2 each way)
Culebra (1 hr ferry from Ceiba, $2 each way)

rincon.title: Rincón
rincon.gettingThere: Fly into SJU or Aguadilla (BQN), then drive or take a shared van about 2 hours west.
highlights: Surf beaches: Domes, Maria's, Sandy Beach

essentials.gettingAround: Car rental essential — town is spread out. Uber not reliable here.

el-yunque.title: El Yunque
el-yunque.tagline: The Only Tropical Rainforest in the US National Forest System
el-yunque.gettingThere: About 45 minutes east of San Juan via PR-3. Entrance off PR-191.

essentials.gettingAround: Car required. Day trip from San Juan or Fajardo works well.

culebra.title: Culebra
culebra.tagline: Home of Flamenco Beach — One of the World's Best
culebra.gettingThere: Ferry from Ceiba (1 hour, $2) or small plane from SJU via Cape Air (20 min).

essentials.gettingAround: Golf carts and bikes for rent on island. Ferry and water taxis to outer cays.
essentials.airport: Culebra Airport (CPX) — small prop planes only

vieques.title: Vieques
vieques.gettingThere: Ferry from Ceiba (1.5 hours, $2) or small plane from SJU via Cape Air (25 min).

essentials.gettingAround: Car or golf cart rental essential. Little public transport.
essentials.airport: Antonio Rivera Rodríguez (VQS) — small prop planes only

ponce.title: Ponce
ponce.tagline: La Perla del Sur — The Pearl of the South
ponce.gettingThere: About 90 minutes south of San Juan via PR-52 (Luis A. Ferré Highway — La Pista).

essentials.gettingAround: Car rental recommended. Publicos (shared vans) connect major routes.

mayaguez.title: Mayagüez
mayaguez.gettingThere: About 2 hours west of San Juan via PR-2, or fly into Aguadilla (BQN) — 30 min north.

essentials.gettingAround: Car rental recommended for exploring the west coast.

arecibo.title: Arecibo
arecibo.tagline: Gateway to Puerto Rico's North Coast
arecibo.gettingThere: About 90 minutes west of San Juan via PR-22 (José de Diego Highway).

essentials.gettingAround: Car required — no reliable public transport to main attractions.

travelTips.currency: US Dollar (USD). No currency exchange needed for US travelers.
travelTips.visaFree: No passport required for US citizens. US territories — travel as domestic.
travelTips.bestOverallMonths: December to April (dry season). Peak: mid-December through April.
travelTips.hurricaneSeason: June to November. Peak risk: August–October. Check forecasts.
travelTips.simCard: US carriers work with existing plans. T-Mobile and AT&T have strong coverage.
travelTips.uber: Uber works well in San Juan metro area. Car rental needed elsewhere.
